definition: Grain shocks transmit to livestock and poultry costs with a 6-12 month lag through feed cost channels (feed represents 60-70% of poultry costs, 50-60% of pork), systematically extending food inflation episodes and making carry re-entry signals based on commodity price reversion premature by 2-3 quarters.
evidence: Production cycle timings are biological constants: poultry ~6 weeks, pork ~5-6 months, cattle ~18-24 months
2022 episode: global grain peaked May-June 2022, US food-at-home CPI peaked August 2022, EM food CPI kept rising through Q1 2023
BLS data by food category shows the cascade clearly
open_questions: Carry trade timing application extrapolation needs more validation
Primarily applicable to EM currencies where protein constitutes large and growing food CPI share
